turns.user_message: Someone casually brushed off my worries yesterday and said I was making a mountain out of a molehill, and I just could not let it go. I keep replaying that exact moment in my head while I try to do basic chores around my place. I feel this tight knot in my chest that just will not loosen up no matter how many deep breaths I take. My thoughts keep circling back to whether I actually messed up or if I am just too sensitive. I just want to shake off the feeling, but it lingers like a heavy fog.

That same comment keeps echoing in my mind every time I try to relax, turning quiet evenings into a loop of second-guessing myself. I catch myself analyzing every little choice I make just to prove I am not being irrational. The physical tension has settled into my shoulders now, and I find myself pacing around the room for hours. It feels like I am constantly waiting for the other shoe to drop, even when everything is perfectly fine. I just wish I could flip a switch and stop letting those words dictate my mood.

I am completely trapped inside that conversation now, and every single interaction I have feels like a minefield I have to navigate. My heart pounds so hard I can hear it in my ears whenever I remember exactly how they looked at me. I start sweating over tiny details and convince myself that I ruined everything before it even begins. The constant buzzing in my head has taken over completely, leaving me exhausted and completely drained of any normal energy. I just want it to stop, but the echo of those words refuses to fade into the background.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did.
